returned_text:
# Markdown Structure Verification

This document exists to confirm that the BlarAI guest-homed parser preserves document structure when it extracts an article. The extraction stage now passes the include_formatting flag to trafilatura, which means headings, unordered lists, and inline emphasis all survive the trip from raw HTML into the cleaned article text that the assistant renders.

## Why structure matters

A knowledge bank that flattens every heading into indistinguishable prose loses the shape of the source. Section boundaries, enumerated steps, and the emphasis an author placed on a **critical warning** are part of the meaning, not decoration. Preserving them keeps the stored article faithful to what was published.

## What this fixture checks

The list below should return with leading dash markers, the headings above should remain distinct lines, and the bold phrase should be wrapped in emphasis markers when the parser returns its cleaned text.

- First, that unordered list items keep their dash markers intact.
- Second, that multiple list entries each stay on their own line.
- Third, that the list is not collapsed into one flat paragraph.

If the cleaned text contains the dash-prefixed list items and the emphasis markers, the include_formatting flag is confirmed live inside the NIC-less guest, end to end over the vsock parse channel.
